title: Relapse Prevention Plan Checklist
description: Use this checklist to build and maintain your personal relapse prevention plan. Having these elements in place before a crisis gives you a clear roadmap when your judgment may be compromised by a mood episode. Review and update this plan regularly — ideally with your treatment team.
instructions: Work through each item. Check off the ones you have in place. For items that aren't checked, consider making them a priority. This is a living document — come back to it periodically to update your plan as your understanding of your patterns deepens.

items.label: I have a written list of my personal early warning signs for depression
I have a written list of my personal early warning signs for mania/hypomania
I have written 'If/Then' action plans for depressive warning signs
I have written 'If/Then' action plans for manic/hypomanic warning signs
I have identified at least one trusted person who knows my warning signs
My trusted person(s) know what I want them to do if they notice warning signs
I have my prescriber's contact information readily accessible
I have a therapist or counselor, or I know how to access one quickly
I have crisis resource numbers saved in my phone (988, Crisis Text Line, local ER)
I know which emergency room I would go to and how to get there
I have financial safeguards in place for manic episodes (spending limits, trusted person on accounts)
I maintain a consistent sleep schedule as a foundation of stability
I have a reliable medication routine (same time daily, reminders if needed)
I track my mood, sleep, and energy regularly (daily or near-daily)
I am mindful about alcohol and substance use and their effect on my mood
I have at least one stress management strategy I use regularly

disclaimer: This checklist supports personal relapse prevention planning. It is not a substitute for professional treatment or a formal psychiatric advance directive. Review your plan with your treatment team. If you are currently in a mood episode or crisis, contact your prescriber or call 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline).
